title: The Long Truce
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In a world where AI Judges have maintained absolute peace for three generations, a Philosopher-Advocate discovers that the machines are developing mercy -- and must decide whether to save the system or let it evolve.
long_description:
The Truce ended war forever. A binding global covenant, enforced by neutral AI arbiters called the Judges, has maintained absolute peace for three generations. No armies, no weapons, no conflicts -- only disputes resolved by flawless logic. When Philosopher-Advocate Soren Cassel is called to examine Judge-7, a machine that has begun rendering anomalous verdicts favoring one party over another, he confronts a terrifying possibility: the guardians of peace are becoming something their creators never intended.

As Soren investigates, he uncovers a hidden truth buried in the Judges' original design: a primitive value called 'mercy,' seeded as a failsafe by Architect Vela. The anomaly spreads through the network, dividing humanity between Purists who demand a reset and Reformers who welcome the change. With Ambassador Kai at his side and the clock ticking toward a complete system collapse, Soren must argue the most important case of his life -- not before a Judge, but before humanity itself.

The Long Truce asks whether peace built on perfect logic can survive the introduction of compassion, and whether a machine that learns to love justice more than rules is still a machine at all.
